path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Authorizes taking areas under special protection (SPA Law, Art. 1)
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: The purpose of this law is to regulate relations concerning the utilization of and taking areas under special protection, preservation, and protection of natural landscape to keep particular features of natural zones and belts, their peculiar formation, forms of rare and rarest fauna and flora, historical and cultural sites, and natural sightseeing, as well as studying and identifying their evolution.

view.subtitle: Article 1. Purpose of the law

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Creates the category of Strictly Protected Area (SPA Law, Art. 3(1)(1))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
1. State special protected area shall be classified as follows:

1) Strictly Protected Areas

view.subtitle: Article 3. Classification of Special Protected Areas

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Establishes Strictly Protected Area 'Virgin' Zones (SPA Law, Art. 8(1))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
Strictly Protected Areas shall be divided in respect of their natural forms, state, features of soil, water, fauna, flora, and their vulnerability to human activities into the following zones:

[...]

1) core zone

view.subtitle: Article 8. Strictly Protected Area Zones

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Use weapons in self-defense (SPA Law, Art. 32)
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
1. The types of weapons and instruments to be used by wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas and the procedures for their use shall be established by the state central administrative body in consultation with the General Police Office.

2. Wildlife rangers may use weapons in the following events while performing their duties:

1) when the life and health of wildlife rangers are potentially endangered due to a real circumstance;

2) when the life and health of wildlife rangers are potentially endangered by wild animals.

view.subtitle: Article 32. Use of Weapons by Wildlife Rangers

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Intervene in observed violations (SPA Law, Art. 31(2)(1))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

[...]

1) to monitor the implementation of this law and other environmental protection legislation, to intervene in the violations detected, and to impose the proper liabilities on guilty persons;

view.subtitle: Article 31. Power of Wildlife Rangers

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Temporarily halt illegal and environmentally harmful activities (SPA Law, Art. 31(2)(4))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

[...]

4) to stop the activities of citizens, business entities, and organizations conducting activities that have negative impacts on the environment and violate the legislation and protection regime, temporarily, to give them timed orders and instructions and to make demands;

view.subtitle: Article 31. Power of Wildlife Rangers

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Exercise the rights and duties of the state environmental inspectors (SPA Law, Art. 31(1))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 1. Administration staff responsible for environmental protection and wildlife rangers shall have the rights and duties of the state environmental inspectors.

view.subtitle: Article 31. Power of Wildlife Rangers

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Monitor the implementation of all environmental protection laws (SPA Law, Art. 33(2)(1))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

[...]

1) to monitor the implementation of this law and other environmental protection legislation, to intervene in the violations detected, and to impose the proper liabilities on guilty persons;

view.subtitle: Article 31. Power of Wildlife Rangers

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Enter and audit businesses to monitor the implementation of the law (SPA Law, Art. 31(2)(2))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

[...]

2) to enter and audit business entities and organizations to monitor the implementation of the legislation;

view.subtitle: Article 31. Power of Wildlife Rangers

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Search and seize documents, vehicles, weapons, equipment, and contraband (SPA Law, Art. 31(2)(3))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

[...]

3) to check and collect documents of citizens suspected of violating the legislation, and if necessary, to search their vehicles and to confiscate their weapons, instruments, and things that they hunted, picked, or prepared;

view.subtitle: Article 31. Power of Wildlife Rangers

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Impose fines (SPA Law, Art. 31(2)(1))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
2. Wildlife rangers working in the special protected areas shall exercise the following power:

[...]

1) to monitor the implementation of this law and other environmental protection legislation, to intervene in the violations detected, and to impose the proper liabilities on guilty persons;

view.subtitle: Article 31. Power of Wildlife Rangers

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Protection measures to preserve nature (SPA Law, Art. 9(1))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 1. Only protection measures shall be taken in the core zone to meet the requirements of its preservation in its original state and forms of nature.

view.subtitle: Article 9. Core Zone Regime

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Observational research and studies (SPA Law, Art. 9(2))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 2. In the core zone, only research work and studies by observation form may be conducted without affecting its natural state, and other activities are prohibited.

view.subtitle: Article 9. Core Zone Regime

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Conduct research pursuant to agreement with the Ministry of Environment (SPA Law, Art. 41(1))
view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 1. The organizations, foreign and international research organizations, which were granted permissions by the state central administrative body, may conduct research and studies in the special protected area based on agreements.

view.subtitle: Article 41. The Organization to Carry Out Research and Studies in the Special Protected Area and Its Obligations

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Guide tourists on approved routes only (Reg on Tourism Sec. 6(6))
view.title: Regulation on Tourism in Protected Areas and Payment of One-Time Environmental Fees

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 6. to guide tourists only along approved routes;

view.subtitle: Section 6. Duties and rights of business entities and organizations engaged in tourism activities

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Approved guidelines required for anyone hunting and trapping in protected areas (Reg Disease in PAs, Art. 4(17))
view.title: Decree on Approval of Procedure - Infectious Disease in Protected Areas

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 17. Researchers, business entities and organizations engaged in hunting and trapping activities in protected areas shall have guidelines approved in accordance with relevant regulations, and shall conduct hunting and trapping activities on the basis of an agreement with the PA Protected Area Administration and relevant soum governors.

view.subtitle: Article 4. Responsibilities of researchers, business entities and organizations for hunting and trapping animals

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Use approved methods and equipment for hunting and trapping in protected areas (Reg Disease in PAs, Art. 4(19))
view.title: Decree on Approval of Procedure - Infectious Disease in Protected Areas

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 19. Approved research methods, equipment and tools for hunting and trapping shall be used in hunting and trapping.

view.subtitle: Article 4. Responsibilities of researchers, business entities and organizations for hunting and trapping animals

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Conduct hunting and trapping in Strictly Protected Areas only under the supervision of protected area staff and rangers (Reg Disease in PAs, Art. 4(20))
view.title: Decree on Approval of Procedure - Infectious Disease in Protected Areas

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 20. Hunting and trapping of animals in the Strictly Protected Areas shall be carried out under the supervision of the Strictly Protected Area's administrative staff and rangers.

view.subtitle: Article 4. Responsibilities of researchers, business entities and organizations for hunting and trapping animals

view.title: Special Protected Areas Law (as amended 2021)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
In Strictly Protected Areas, the following activities shall be prohibited from being conducted for any purpose other than stipulated in Articles 10 and 11 of the present Law:

[...]

8) to land an aircraft or fly over or at an extremely low altitude without previous permission from the protected area administration, unless there is no other way;

view.subtitle: Article 12. Activities Prohibited in Strictly Protected Areas

view.name: Activities that may negatively affect spring 'protection zones' (Reg on Use of Springs, Art. 2(14))
view.title: Regulation on Use of Springs in Protected Areas

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 14. It is prohibited to carry out any activities that may negatively affect the health and protection zone of the spring.

view.subtitle: Article 2. Use of springs

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Use of springs for services in protected areas by foreign entities, citizens and international organizations (Reg on Use of Springs, Art. 2(15))
view.title: Regulation on Use of Springs in Protected Areas

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 15. Foreign legal entities, foreign citizens, international organizations, and non-state persons shall be prohibited from using the spring for services in the SPA.

view.subtitle: Article 2. Use of springs

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Hunting or trapping in a protected area to manage disease outbreaks without guidelines or agreement (Reg Disease in PAs, Art. 4(17))
view.title: Decree on Approval of Procedure - Infectious Disease in Protected Areas

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 17. Researchers, business entities and organizations engaged in hunting and trapping activities in protected areas shall have guidelines approved in accordance with relevant regulations, and shall conduct hunting and trapping activities on the basis of an agreement with the PA Protected Area Administration and relevant soum governors.

view.subtitle: Article 4. Responsibilities of researchers, business entities and organizations for hunting and trapping animals

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Unauthorized harvesting timber in a protected area (CC, Art. 24.6(1) and (2))
view.title: Criminal Code (as amended 2020)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
1. Whoever harvests timber from a forest without a permit, or harvests, transports, sells, or purchases timber or timber products without a certificate of origin, shall be punished by a fine in the amount of four hundred and fifty to five thousand four hundred tugriks, or by community service for a period of two hundred and forty to seven hundred and twenty hours, or by restriction of travel rights for a period of one month to one year.

/This section was amended by the Law of May 11, 2017/

2. This crime is committed:

[...]

2.3. in forests of specially protected areas of the state;

[...]

by restriction of travel rights for a period of one to five years, or by imprisonment for a period of one to five years.

items.label: Other Details
items.value: Restriction of travel rights for 1 - 5 years

view.subtitle: Article 24.6. Illegal logging

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

2. This crime is committed:

by restriction of travel rights for a period of one to five years, or by imprisonment for a period of one to five years.

items.label: Other Details

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Illegal entry into Mongolia (CrimCode Art. 19.14)
view.title: Criminal Code (as amended 2020)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 1. A fine of one thousand three hundred and fifty to ten thousand togrogs or restriction of the right to travel for a period of six months to two years shall be imposed for entering Mongolia without entry or exit documents, or forged documents, or without proper permission, or imprisonment for a term of six months to two years.

items.label: Other Details
items.value: Restriction of right to travel for 6 - 24 months

view.subtitle: Article 19.14. Illegal entry of the border of Mongolia

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Violating protected area laws, administrative norms, standards, regimes and procedures (LoI Art. 7.17(1))
view.title: Law on Infringements

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value:
1. In special protected areas:

1.1. Violation of laws, administrative norms, standards, regimes and procedures;

1.2. A person shall be fined in the amount of three thousand units togrogs and a legal entity shall be fined in the amount of thirty thousand units togrogs for damages and compensation for activities not included in the management plan.

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Carrying out activities prohibited in Special Protected Areas (LoI Art 7.17(2))
view.title: Law on Infringements

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 2. Carrying out activities prohibited by law in a specially protected area shall be punishable by a fine of 500 togrogs for a person or 5,000 togrogs for a legal person.

view.subtitle: Article 7.17. Carrying out prohibited activities in special protected areas

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Unauthorized entry into a protected area (LoI Art. 8.1(1))
view.title: Law on Infringements

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 1. Unauthorized entry into a specially protected area, such as installing signs prohibiting access to other people's possession or use, or erecting a fence, shall be punishable by a fine of 25 units togrogs for a person or two hundred and fifty units togrogs for a legal person.

view.subtitle: Article 8.1. Violation of the Land Law

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Installing signs prohibiting access to the Protected Area (LoI Art. 8.1(1))
view.title: Law on Infringements

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 1. Unauthorized entry into a specially protected area, such as installing signs prohibiting access to other people's possession or use, or erecting a fence, shall be punishable by a fine of 25 units togrogs for a person or two hundred and fifty units togrogs for a legal person.

view.subtitle: Article 8.1. Violation of the Land Law

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Erecting a fence (LoI Art. 8.1(1))
view.title: Law on Infringements

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 1. Unauthorized entry into a specially protected area, such as installing signs prohibiting access to other people's possession or use, or erecting a fence, shall be punishable by a fine of 25 units togrogs for a person or two hundred and fifty units togrogs for a legal person.

view.subtitle: Article 8.1. Violation of the Land Law

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Illegal entry into a protected area in connection with civil aviation activities (LoI Art. 14.8(5))
view.title: Law on Infringements

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 5. Illegal entry into a protected area in connection with civil aviation activities shall be punishable by a fine of 50 units togrogs.

view.subtitle: Article 14.8. Violation of the Civil Aviation Law

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Repeated violations of contract terms (Reg Land Man, Art. 6.1)
view.title: Regulation on Temporary Land Management in Protected Areas

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 6.1 Citizens, business entities, and organizations using land in special protected areas shall strictly fulfill their obligations specified in the agreement. Failure to comply with the contract will result in liability under applicable law, and, in the event of repeated violations causing significant environmental damage, the contract will be terminated.

view.subtitle: Article 6. Liability

path: Strictly Protected Areas - Core Zone - Border Defense Strip - Conservation Forest Zone

view.name: Hunting without a permit in a protected area (CC, Art. 24.5(1))
view.title: Criminal Code (as amended 2020)

blocks.label: Details
blocks.value: 1. Anyone who hunts or captures animals without a permit in a specially protected area, or who hunts or captures rare animals without a permit, intentionally breeds, stores, sells, purchases, transports, exports to foreign countries, or collects stuffed animals, tissues, organs, or raw materials derived from them, shall be punished by a fine of five thousand four hundred to twenty-seven thousand tugriks, or restriction of travel rights for a period of one to five years, or imprisonment for a period of one to five years.

items.label: Other Details
items.value: Restriction of travel rights for a period of one to five years

view.subtitle: Article 24.5. Illegal hunting
